checks.finding: No issues found. T-001/T-009/T-010 (low) touch only inert scaffolding, read-only metric aggregation, and doc/test-expectation sync respectively -- no sentinel surface. T-002/T-003/T-004/T-007/T-008 (medium) touch generation logic, agent-file additions, and warn-only gates with no auth/payment/PII/migration/externally-visible-API-contract surface; T-004's status-field reset is an internal state transition without approval authority, consistent with its Risk Rationale. T-005/T-006 (high) correctly reflect authorization/approval-gate surfaces (T-005: review/approval-adjacent governance infrastructure; T-006: hook-guard authorization boundary) per risk-classification-policy.md's 'high' tier definition.

No issues found. The two Risk: high tasks (T-005, T-006) both list 'Red-Green evidence captured' and 'Independent review verdict recorded' as explicit Done When items. No Risk: critical task exists in tasks.md, so the second-approver/signed-evidence-bundle requirement does not apply.

T-005 ('domain-review-loop, domain-reviewer-a/b, and cross-model-verify wiring') spans more than three distinct implementation areas in its Scope paragraph: (1) the domain-review-loop state machine, (2) two independent reviewer agent definitions plus a calibration reference (3 separate files), (3) the cross-model-verify invocation with mismatch/unavailable handling, and (4) the AC-014 drift-detection precheck. The task title itself names three separate subsystems ('domain-review-loop, domain-reviewer-a/b, and cross-model-verify'), matching the oversizing indicator of a title implying multiple sequential/parallel build phases. Splitting the drift-precheck (AC-014) and/or the cross-model-verify wiring (AC-006/AC-017) into a separate task from the core two-reviewer round loop (AC-005) would keep each task within one coherent implementation area.

AC-004 (seed intake for domain-interviewer, owned by T-002) has only a happy-path test in acceptance-tests.md: TEST-004 covers 'seeds: text, Markdown path, issue URL; reverse consumes investigation.md' with no error-path case, even though ux-spec.md's Component States table explicitly documents an Error state for this same component ('seed unreadable / URL unreachable' -> 'plain-language error + which seed failed' -> 're-supply seed; never invent content', REQ-003/AC-004). T-002's Done When list also contains no fixture or test task item covering an unreadable seed or unreachable URL -- only the eight-artifact-path fixture and the stage-resume fixture. The documented error path in ux-spec.md has no corresponding acceptance-test or task-level verification evidence.

No issues found. Unit tests (TEST-001, TEST-003, TEST-007, TEST-009, TEST-011, TEST-013, TEST-014, TEST-015) each target a single script/module boundary (schema validator, hook guard, conformance script, template set, validate-repository expectations). Integration tests (TEST-002, TEST-004, TEST-005, TEST-006, TEST-008, TEST-010, TEST-012, TEST-016, TEST-017) each involve at least two components (interviewer+templates+contract, interviewer+reverse+investigate-codebase, two reviewer agents+aggregation, cross-model panel wiring, domain-sync+bootstrap Phase 1, whole-workflow absence regression, quality-gate report+retrospective aggregation, multi-stage update-mode re-run). No AC-NNN-labeled acceptance-type test is missing its AC reference; all 17 rows cite an AC-NNN.

Neither Risk: high task (T-005, T-006) contains a `Rollback:` field or a Done When item addressing safe reversion. tasks.md has zero occurrences of 'Rollback', 'revert', or 'feature flag' anywhere in the document. infra-spec.md's Rollback section only covers a whole-plugin-directory revert at the repository level and does not address T-005 (review-loop/cross-model gate state) or T-006 (hook-guard authorization extension shared with the existing tasks.md/WFI guard code paths) individually as required at the task level; neither task's Done When references that spec-level plan or names a task-specific reversion mechanism (e.g., a flag to disable the new guard regex, or a documented-and-tested rollback step for the review-loop wiring).

No issues found. The only file shared across tasks' Planned Files is the three sdd-domain plugin manifests, created by T-001 and later amended (skill entry added) by T-004; this ordering is enforced transitively via Blockers (T-004 -> T-002 -> T-001, and T-004 -> T-003 -> T-002 -> T-001). No two tasks claim to modify the same primary implementation file for the same purpose without a Blockers relationship.

Two spurious/inconsistent blockers found. (1) T-003's Blockers lists 'T-002', but T-003's own Goal and Scope state it 'produces a seed structure consumable by T-002's interviewer' -- i.e. T-002 is the downstream consumer of T-003's output (matching design.md Architecture's DR -> DI edge), not a prerequisite T-003 depends on; T-003's only real input is the already-existing `investigate-codebase` skill. Blocking T-003 on T-002 inverts the actual data-dependency direction and inflates the critical path. (2) T-004's Blockers lists 'T-005', but T-004's own Out of Scope section explicitly states 'The review/approval gate (T-005) ... ' is excluded from this task, and neither of T-004's Done When test items (`domain-model-visibility.Tests.ps1`, `update-mode.Tests.ps1`) exercises domain-review-loop. Declaring T-005 both out of scope and a hard blocker for T-004 is an internal contradiction that inflates the critical path without a demonstrated artifact dependency.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All ten tasks (T-001 through T-010) are new-feature build tasks for the sdd-domain plugin; none is scoped as a bugfix, regression fix, debugging, failure-diagnosis, flaky-test-resolution, or incident-remediation task by title, Goal, Scope, or Done When text.
